schema_description: Structured heuristic packs for the UX Review Copilot. Covers AI-generated and user-imported UI quality review. Each rule maps to an NNG heuristic, a severity default, a LeafyGreen recommendation, and a check_prompt for the AI reviewer.

packs.description: Fundamental UI completeness checks grounded in Jakob Nielsen's 10 Usability Heuristics. Catches missing states, broken flows, weak error handling, and recovery path gaps — the most common failures in AI-generated UI.

rules.title: Loading state present for async actions
rules.nng_principle: The design should always keep users informed about what is going on, through appropriate feedback within a reasonable amount of time.
rules.check_prompt: Is there a visible loading or pending state when an async action (form submit, data fetch, save) is triggered? Or does the UI appear frozen or unchanged?

rules.title: Empty state defined for zero-data views
rules.nng_principle: Users need to understand current system state, including when there is nothing to show.
rules.check_prompt: Does every list, table, or data view have an empty state for when no data exists? Does it explain why and provide a clear next action?

rules.title: Destructive actions require confirmation
rules.nng_principle: Prevent high-cost errors by presenting users with a confirmation option before they commit to an action.
rules.check_prompt: Do any destructive or irreversible actions (delete, remove, reset, overwrite) require a confirmation step before executing? Is the consequence clearly communicated?

rules.title: Success feedback present and persistent enough to register
rules.nng_principle: Users need confirmation that their action completed successfully.
rules.check_prompt: Is there visible success feedback after a completed action (save, submit, send)? Is it persistent enough for the user to register it before it disappears?

rules.title: Error state present with plain-language recovery guidance
rules.nng_heuristic: H9 — Help Users Recognize, Diagnose, and Recover from Errors
rules.nng_principle: Error messages should be expressed in plain language, precisely indicate the problem, and constructively suggest a solution.
rules.check_prompt: Are error states shown for failed actions or system errors? Do they explain what went wrong in plain language (no error codes) and provide a recovery path?
rules.leafygreen_component: Banner (variant: danger), Callout (variant: error), Toast (variant: warning)

rules.title: Cancel or undo path is clearly available
rules.nng_principle: Users often perform actions by mistake and need a clearly marked emergency exit without going through an extended process.
rules.check_prompt: Can users cancel in-progress actions or undo recent changes? Is the cancel/undo path clearly labeled and easily discoverable — not buried or absent?
rules.leafygreen_component: Form Footer (cancel action), Button (variant: default for Cancel)

rules.title: Incomplete flows have visible next-step guidance
rules.nng_principle: When documentation is necessary, it should be focused on the user's task, presented in context, and list concrete steps.
rules.check_prompt: After completing a step in a multi-step flow, is the next step clearly communicated? Does the interface guide users forward, or does it leave them at a dead end?

rules.title: Labels and terminology match user language
rules.nng_heuristic: H2 — Match Between System and Real World
rules.nng_principle: The design should speak the user's language using words, phrases, and concepts familiar to the user, not internal jargon.
rules.check_prompt: Are labels, button text, and section headings written in plain user-facing language? Are there cases of internal jargon, technical shorthand, or terminology inconsistency across the same flow?
rules.leafygreen_component: Inline Definition, Info Sprinkle for first-use terminology

rules.title: UI noise is not competing with the primary task
rules.nng_principle: Every extra unit of information competes with relevant information and diminishes its relative visibility.
rules.check_prompt: Is there visible UI noise — secondary actions, decorative elements, or low-priority information — that competes with the primary task or goal of this screen?
rules.leafygreen_component: No specific component — refer to MongoDB minimalist design foundations

packs.description: Checks for accessible feedback patterns, semantic clarity, and non-color-only communication. Grounded in NNG heuristics and MongoDB accessibility foundations.

rules.title: Status not communicated by color alone
rules.nng_principle: System status must be communicated through means accessible to all users.
rules.check_prompt: Does any status, error, or state change rely solely on color to communicate meaning? Is a secondary indicator (icon, label, or text) also present alongside the color change?
rules.leafygreen_component: Badge, Banner, Toast, Callout — all include icons alongside color by default in LeafyGreen

rules.title: Interactive controls have visible descriptive labels
rules.nng_heuristic: H2 — Match Between System and Real World
rules.nng_principle: Labels should use words and concepts familiar to the user.
rules.check_prompt: Do all buttons, inputs, toggles, and interactive elements have clear visible labels? Are icon-only controls supplemented with tooltips or accessible names?
rules.leafygreen_component: Icon Button (requires aria-label), Tooltip for icon-only buttons

rules.title: Focus order appears logical from visible structure
rules.nng_heuristic: H7 — Flexibility and Efficiency of Use
rules.nng_principle: Flexible processes should be accessible to users navigating without a mouse.
rules.check_prompt: From the visible UI structure, does the likely focus order appear logical (top-to-bottom, left-to-right, modal-trapped where appropriate)? Are any obvious focus traps or dead ends visible?
rules.leafygreen_component: All LeafyGreen interactive components include keyboard navigation support by default

rules.title: Form fields have associated visible labels
rules.nng_principle: Information required to use the design should be visible or easily retrievable when needed.
rules.check_prompt: Do all form inputs have a visible label associated with them? Are placeholder-only fields present (which fail accessibility requirements when labels disappear on focus)?
rules.leafygreen_component: Text Input, Text Area, Select, Combobox — all include label slots in LeafyGreen

packs.description: Checks for LeafyGreen component alignment, MongoDB terminology consistency, and product pattern conventions. Use when reviewing MongoDB Atlas, Cloud Manager, or any internal MongoDB tooling.

rules.title: Custom components used where LeafyGreen equivalent exists
rules.nng_principle: Follow platform and industry conventions to reduce cognitive load.
rules.check_prompt: Are any custom-designed UI components used where a LeafyGreen equivalent clearly exists? Flag reimplemented Toasts, Banners, Modals, Badges, Empty States, or form components.
rules.leafygreen_component: Refer to full component library at mongodb.design

rules.title: MongoDB terminology used consistently
rules.nng_principle: Users should not have to wonder whether different words mean the same thing.
rules.check_prompt: Does the UI use MongoDB-standard terms (Project, Organization, Cluster, Atlas, etc.) consistently? Are there cases of inconsistent capitalization, mixed synonyms, or terminology that conflicts with the MongoDB product glossary?
rules.leafygreen_component: Inline Definition, Info Sprinkle for first-use or complex terms

rules.title: Navigation follows Cloud Nav Layout patterns
rules.nng_principle: Navigation structure should match platform conventions users have learned from the rest of the product.
rules.check_prompt: Does the navigation structure align with MongoDB's Cloud Nav Layout pattern? Are Side Nav and Section Nav components used for primary and secondary navigation rather than ad-hoc custom patterns?
rules.leafygreen_component: Cloud Nav Layout, Side Nav, Section Nav

rules.title: Copy follows MongoDB voice and tone
rules.nng_heuristic: H2 — Match Between System and Real World
rules.nng_principle: The design should speak the user's language using words and concepts familiar to the user.
rules.check_prompt: Does UI copy feel consistent with MongoDB's voice (clear, direct, human)? Are there overly technical error messages, passive constructions, vague CTAs ('Submit' instead of 'Create Cluster'), or tone inconsistencies?
rules.leafygreen_component: Refer to Copy: Voice and Tone guidelines

rules.title: Empty and access restriction states use MongoDB patterns
rules.nng_principle: Documentation should be focused on the user's task and presented in context.
rules.check_prompt: Are empty states and access restriction messages using MongoDB's dedicated patterns rather than custom treatments? Do they follow the MongoDB empty state structure (illustration, heading, description, CTA)?
rules.leafygreen_component: Empty State, Access Restriction Messages pattern, Feature Walls

packs.description: Configurable pack for team-specific review rules. Copy this pack and define rules that reflect your team's design system expectations, workflow constraints, or product-specific heuristics. Rules defined here are injected into the review at runtime.

rules.title: [Rule title — short and specific]
rules.nng_heuristic: [Optional: map to an NNG heuristic number and name]
rules.nng_principle: [Optional: quote or paraphrase the heuristic principle]
rules.check_prompt: [1–2 sentences describing exactly what the AI reviewer should look for or ask about in the artifact.]
rules.leafygreen_component: [Recommended LeafyGreen component, or 'None — custom pattern required' with reason]
rules.leafygreen_url: [Link to relevant component on mongodb.design, or omit if not applicable]
